events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario: crew proposes a shortcut for panelboard installation & makeup, but the submittal omits the product listing page and project note that would decide whether the shortcut is allowed. Work is in-progress. We need to judge.

Key issue: submittal is incomplete—missing product listing page and project note that determine whether shortcut is allowed. Without that documentation, can't verify compliance. NEC Art. 408 covers panelboards/switchboards, 110.26 working clearance, 200.4 (color coding of grounded conductors? Actually 200.4 is about identification of grounded conductors—no, 200.4 is "Conductors, Grounded, Identification of" — actually 200.4 is about "Grounded conductors" identification? Let me recall: NEC 200.4 is "Grounded Conductors" — no, 200.4 is "Identification of Grounded Conductors" maybe. Actually 200.4 is "Grounded conductors" — hmm. Let me think: NEC Article 200 is "Use and Identification of Grounded Conductors." 200.4 is "Grounded conductors" — actually 200.4 is "Identification of Grounded Conductors." Wait, 200.4 is "Grounded conductors" — I think 200.4 is "Conductors, Grounded, Identification of" — no. Let me not overthink.

The decision: needs_more_info because submittal is incomplete and the shortcut's permissibility depends on missing documentation. Risk medium. Work stage in-progress. Percent complete maybe 40. Confidence moderate.

Findings: submittal missing product listing page and project note; cannot verify shortcut compliance; potential non-compliance with NEC 408 listing requirements and 110.26 clearances.

Actions: obtain complete submittal with product listing and project notes; verify shortcut against listing/installation instructions; do not proceed with shortcut until documentation confirms compliance; coordinate with inspector/AHJ.

References: NEC Art. 408, 110.26, 200.4.

Let me write the JSON.
